2026-02-20.thesis: Iran tensions escalate, tanker rates spike on Hormuz risk
2026-02-20.assessment: First week of entry. Tanker rates spiking on Hormuz risk premium, BWET already near-doubled YTD per MarketBeat, indicating strong momentum but also growing mainstream attention (ETF-performance puff piece is a yellow flag for crowding). No concrete resolution of Iran tensions; geopolitical backdrop (bignewsnetwork piece on shift to deterrence) suggests escalation risk remains live, not resolved. No named ceasefire, no reopened chokepoint, no signed deal. Catalyst is still pending/ongoing. Vehicle BWET remains the cleanest pure-play on tanker/freight rates tied to Hormuz risk premium. Conviction moderate — thesis is specific and datable to Hormuz chokepoint risk, but the 'top-performing ETF' coverage signals the trade is becoming known, not deeply under-the-radar anymore.
2026-02-20.exit_advice: Hold BWET; watch for Hormuz de-escalation or diplomatic breakthrough as reversal signal.

2026-02-27.thesis: Iran tensions escalate, tanker rates spike on Hormuz risk
2026-02-27.exit_case: No ceasefire or Hormuz reopening reported; tensions still unresolved, no reversal evidence.
2026-02-27.assessment: Silent week with no fresh news, but silence is not exit per rules. Iran-Hormuz tension remains unresolved and no concrete reversal (ceasefire, chokepoint reopening) has occurred. Catalyst is still pending. Prior journal flagged crowding risk (top-performing ETF puff pieces), but arc is not complete since no named resolution has landed; this is a quiet stretch, not a reversal. Maintain hold on the pure-play tanker rate vehicle.
2026-02-27.exit_advice: Hold BWET; watch for any Hormuz de-escalation, ceasefire, or tanker rate collapse as exit trigger.

2026-03-06.thesis: Iran tensions escalate, tanker rates spike on Hormuz risk
2026-03-06.exit_case: No ceasefire or Hormuz reopening reported; tensions remain unresolved and driving condition intact
2026-03-06.assessment: Third consecutive quiet week with no fresh news, but silence alone is not an exit signal per discipline. Iran-Hormuz risk remains unresolved—no named deal, ceasefire, or chokepoint reopening has occurred in any prior week. The arc is not complete: no concrete de-escalation catalyst has landed, so this is dormancy not resolution. Crowding concern (top-performing ETF coverage) flagged earlier remains a mainstream-attention risk, but without a resolved arc this alone doesn't trigger the herd-arrival exit. Conviction is moderated from initial entry given the trade has drifted from under-the-radar toward more visible momentum-ETF coverage, but the specific geopolitical driver (Hormuz chokepoint tension) has not reversed.
2026-03-06.exit_advice: Hold BWET; exit only on concrete Hormuz de-escalation/ceasefire or if tanker rates roll over on chokepoint reopening

2026-03-13.thesis: Iran tensions escalate, tanker rates spike on Hormuz risk
2026-03-13.exit_case: none - Hormuz tension unresolved, no ceasefire or chokepoint reopening reported
2026-03-13.assessment: Fourth week holding through continued Hormuz risk premium. MarketWatch confirms BWET still climbing on tanker day-rates tied to Mideast tensions, and explicitly characterizes the trade as still niche/under-owned - pushing back against the crowding concern flagged in prior weeks. No concrete resolution (no named deal, ceasefire, or chokepoint reopening) has occurred in any week since entry. Catalyst remains pending and live. 2026-03-20.thesis: Iran tensions escalate, tanker rates spike on Hormuz risk
2026-03-20.exit_case: none — no ceasefire, deal, or chokepoint reopening; Mideast war/inflation fears still escalating per this week's news
2026-03-20.assessment: Fifth week holding. News confirms continued, even escalating, Hormuz/Mideast tension — European shares fell for a second week on 'Mideast war' inflation fears, and FTSE energy names rose on Strait of Hormuz watch. No resolution or reversal has occurred in any week since entry; the driving condition (chokepoint risk premium) remains fully in force. Coverage is now macro-mainstream (war fears cited as a driver of European equity losses), which raises crowding risk, but the arc is not complete — there is no concrete scheduled off-ramp (no summit, no deal signing, no ceasefire date) that would mark the catalyst as resolved. This is still an open-ended escalation, not a herd-arrival-after-resolution situation, so the third exit condition (arc complete + mainstream saturation) is not yet satisfied. BWET remains the cleanest pure-play vehicle on tanker/freight rates tied to Hormuz risk.
2026-03-20.exit_advice: Hold BWET; exit only on a concrete de-escalation (ceasefire, Hormuz reopening, or named deal), not on crowding or price action.

2026-03-27.thesis: Iran tensions escalate, tanker rates spike on Hormuz risk
2026-03-27.exit_case: Oil slumps and equities surge on easing Mideast war fears — Hormuz risk premium reversing.
2026-03-27.assessment: After five weeks of holding through pure escalation/silence, this week brings the first explicit reversal signal: indianexpress.com reports Sensex/Nifty surging as Brent crude slumps 'on optimism over easing geopolitical tensions' — directly opposite the Hormuz risk-premium thesis that BWET depends on. No formal ceasefire or deal was signed, but the driving condition (rising war-risk premium in oil/tanker markets) is explicitly described as reversing in the market's own pricing action. Combined with prior weeks' crowding flags (macro-mainstream war-fear coverage, top-performing ETF pieces), this tips into exit: the market is now pricing de-escalation, which undercuts the tanker day-rate premium that is BWET's entire edge. Holding further risks being long a decaying risk premium after the market has already started to reprice it.
2026-03-27.exit_advice: Exit BWET — first concrete de-escalation signal (oil slump, 'war fears ease' headline) reverses the driving condition; tanker rate premium likely compressing.

2026-04-17.thesis: Iran Hormuz blockade threat keeps tanker rates elevated
2026-04-17.exit_case: none — Iran still threatening blockade, talks ongoing, no resolution or reversal yet
2026-04-17.assessment: First week of entry. Iran's naval threat against Gulf/Oman/Red Sea shipping remains live and unresolved, with talks ongoing but no deal. Tanker day-rates staying elevated confirms the driving condition is intact. BWET is explicitly called the purest pure-play freight-futures vehicle vs diluted tanker operators. Catalyst is still pending (no blockade enacted, no ceasefire/resolution), and coverage is not yet mainstream-saturated — this is still a specific, datable geopolitical risk trade.
2026-04-17.exit_advice: Hold BWET while Hormuz threat persists and day-rates stay elevated; exit if Iran-US talks yield de-escalation deal or blockade threat formally withdrawn

2026-04-24.thesis: Iran Hormuz blockade threat keeps tanker rates elevated
2026-04-24.exit_case: Iran declared Strait of Hormuz open/operational amid ceasefire; blockade threat has reversed.
2026-04-24.assessment: The specific catalyst (Hormuz blockade threat) has been explicitly de-escalated by Iran's own statement that the strait stays operational. This is a driving-condition reversal, not mere quiet. Tanker rate premium built on blockade risk should decompress. Exit the position.
2026-04-24.exit_advice: Exit BWET now; Iran explicitly stated Hormuz remains open during the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire, removing the driving condition (blockade risk) that justified elevated tanker rates. Markets already rallied on this de-escalation (Stoxx 600, Nasdaq streak). No concrete pending catalyst remains, only speculative 'coming energy crisis' commentary.
